title: Fix Docker compat `/wait` hanging for next-exit condition
body:
The issue is caused because the container exists before podman setups wainting for the event. 

Fixes: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/28514

```release-note
Fixed a bug where Docker clients would hang indefinitely on `docker run` when using the Podman Docker-compatible API
```

comments.body:
https://github.com/docker/cli/blob/dea3ebaa87f4a1979e24e82c1b062da5ff5c481c/cli/command/container/run.go#L203-L206

so wait is called before start which seems ok if they use next-exit. But then because the wait call from the client gets the 200 OK right away from us and then continues to start while waiting for the actual wait payload which can come much later.

So now I see why your patch works because you get the state pre the 200 ok status code. But knowing this then I think we can improve this instead by making sure the event subscriber is registered before we send the status code and then it should no longer miss the exit event as docker only starts once it has the 200 status code response
(oh and would definitely be good to get this context in the commit message)
That is a much cleaner approach. I have made the changes.
tmt tests failed for commit ecf493dea05e221552ce1ac43c68b8acbd9ce3ba. @lsm5, @psss, @thrix please check.
